Items.Item: Rarity: MAGIC Physician's Quicksilver Flask of the Hare Unique ID: bbdffb8be33810f4dd4c6a3b110fa4e6125f594d2bd3fd404cd56356c6f312f8 Item Level: 76 Quality: 0 LevelReq: 20 Implicits: 1 {crafted}Used when you Hit a Rare or Unique Enemy, if not already in effect 19% chance to gain a Flask Charge when you deal a Critical Strike 7% increased Movement Speed during Effect

Rarity: RARE Damnation Nails Wyrmscale Gauntlets Armour: 117 ArmourBasePercentile: 0.5434 Evasion: 117 EvasionBasePercentile: 0.5434 Unique ID: d64cbb558052f2ef52e6e15d824b73fa7222d82c8609ee49722558f9835d4667 Searing Exarch Item Eater of Worlds Item Item Level: 69 Quality: 0 Sockets: R-B-G-G LevelReq: 49 Implicits: 2 Inflict Cold Exposure on Hit, applying -11% to Cold Resistance 7% reduced Enemy Stun Threshold +6% chance to Suppress Spell Damage 21% increased Armour and Evasion +93 to maximum Life +21% to Chaos Resistance 10% increased Stun and Block Recovery {crafted}10% increased Attack Speed

Rarity: UNIQUE Carnage Heart Onyx Amulet Unique ID: 0ebc77d43c1ee79d568fda15321cfb751f7bfa356ae65bebb34a33a91db7fe25 Item Level: 71 LevelReq: 20 Implicits: 2 {crafted}Allocates Divine Judgement +19 to all Attributes +39 to all Attributes +20% to all Elemental Resistances 1.71% of Physical Attack Damage Leeched as Life 50% increased total Recovery per second from Life Leech 34% increased Damage while Leeching Extra gore

Rarity: RARE Kraken Urge Slink Boots Evasion: 272 EvasionBasePercentile: 0.7027 Unique ID: d8d73be6044a5684b5da05642836c12ec5d6668d394bdb6d4adb11c44b10cb0f Searing Exarch Item Eater of Worlds Item Item Level: 75 Quality: 0 Sockets: R-R-B G LevelReq: 69 Implicits: 2 Regenerate 0.2% of Life per second per Endurance Charge +1% to maximum Fire Resistance +33% to Fire Resistance +35% to Lightning Resistance +25% to Chaos Resistance 30% increased Movement Speed {crafted}+62 to maximum Life

PathOfBuilding.Notes:
^xAF6025^x70FF70^xFFFF77Levelling^7
^7All of these tips assume a fresh start, or at least not using a dedicated reroll build (like Hollow Palm.) If you have another levelling setup you feel more comfortable with you can definitely do that. PConc could also be good on a fresh start but as I'm not familiar with that I don't want to share questionable information about it. The important thing is that you have fun while efficiently clearing the campaign.
If you wanted to mule ^xE05030Smite^7 you could kill Hillock on a Templar and then get the quest reward before starting your duelist. This only matters for a fresh start, and I haven't tested it myself so I can't speak to how good it would feel, but it's an option.
Act 1: ^x70FF70Spectral Throw ^7- ^xE05030Added Fire Damage ^7- ^x70FF70Faster Attacks ^x7070FFFrostblink ^xE05030Leap Slam ^7- ^x70FF70Faster Attacks ^x70FF70Sniper's Mark ^xE05030Enduring Cry^7
^7Act 2: ^7^x70FF70Herald of Ice Poacher's Mark ^xE05030 ^7Also make sure you pick up ^xE05030Elemental Damage with Attacks ^xC8C8C8^7and^7 ^x7070FFTrinity ^7for levelling in your offhand setup, along with ^xE05030 Flesh and Stone^7 and ^x70FF70Blood Rage^7
Help ^xFFFF77Alira^7 early (you can also choose to ^xFFFF77Kill All Bandits^7 if you don't want to respec but it's hard for 1 point to beat 15 Elemental Resistance this early)
Act 3: ^xE05030Smite ^7- ^xE05030Elemental Damage with Attacks ^7- ^x70FF70Faster Attacks ^7(if you have a 4th link ^x7070FFTrinity ^7is great if you can proc it, otherwise you can use something like ^x70FF70Added Cold Damage^7 / ^xE05030Added Fire Damage ^7/ ^xE05030Rage^7) ^x70FF70Haste^7
Act 4: ^xE05030Smite^7 - ^xE05030Elemental Damage with Attacks ^7- ^xE05030Multistrike ^7- ^x7070FFTrinity ^7/ ^x70FF70Added Cold Damage ^7/ ^xE05030Added Fire Damage ^7(Trinity is best but you have to make sure you're proccing) ^x70FF70Mark on Hit ^7- ^x70FF70Poacher's Mark ^7- ^xE05030Lifetap
^7If you have extra sockets you can also grab a ^xE05030Stone Golem ^7or ^xE05030Fire Golem ^7from the quest reward at the end of the act. It's not a lot of power but it does something with an empty socket at this early stage of the game.
^xFFFF77Ascendancy Order ^7Lab 1 - ^xAF6025Impact^7 Lab 2 - ^xAF6025Brutal Fervour^7 Lab 3 - ^xAF6025Bane of Legends^7 Lab 4 - ^xAF6025Headsman^7
You could also take Brutal Fervour last. Your damage will feel much better but you lose the only defense we get from our Ascendancy so there is a real opportunity cost to this.
^xFFFF77^xFFFF77Early Maps^7 This guide is tailored to trade, but you could absolutely play this build in SSF. You're just going to spend longer getting some of these uniques.
I'll have an early maps gear set with barebones stuff to get you started. Once you're strong enough to break into Tier 16s your immediate priority should be getting ^xAF6025Rakiata's Dance^7. For the price this sword is absolutely unmatched, and can easily carry you through Uber bosses and juiced T17 mod content. If you're on a second character and have the money you can just pick this up right away and shift your focus over to every other piece of gear.
Prior to ^xAF6025Rakiata's Dance^7 I think you're better off using a ^xFFFF77Rare Axe^7. Hatchet Master is so strong early and the Rage generation from the Mastery is incredible until you can easily gain Rage from other sources.
Because of our position on the tree it's trivial to cap Spell Suppression. You can get a low roll on a single piece of gear, Eldritch Implicit on Gloves, or even a craft (body armour is the best spot for this.)
Make sure you check your build in PoB before you switch away from Precise Technique. My build wasn't stronger without it until after I had gotten my first 2 stones, but this was on a fresh start so your experience may vary greatly. The important thing here is to test out your changes here in PoB before you commit to swapping into Crit.
Getting a ^xAF6025Lethal Pride^7 asap is huge, essentially solving our ^xE05030Strength^7 by itself. Try to get some combination of Endurance Charge on Kill, Intimidate on Hit, and Gain Rage on Hit (prioritizing in that order.) If you just have the first 2 nodes that's still an excellent starter jewel, and you can always go back later to get one with more useful nodes. You can also look for jewels to go into the other viable socket we have, so you have quite a few options here. You can use the "Find Timeless Jewel" function at the bottom of the Tree tab here, and I've already plugged in the 3 nodes we care about. You can change the jewel socket to search up at the top, and if you want to drop one of the nodes from being mandatory simply replace the last 1 in that line with a 0.
^xAF6025The Taming^7 is amazing for damage and ele res at the cost of losing life in that slot. I do recommend using it but if you're feeling squishy and want to level you could drop it for a rare ring with a good life roll and whatever other stats you need to fill out your character.
^xAF6025Yoke of Suffering^7 is very similar to ^xAF6025Rakiata's Dance^7. You CAN beat it with a rare but it's incredibly hard to do and would be MUCH more expensive. Because of this you should just pick up Yoke whenever you can afford it and ignore that slot until you're very deep into the endgame.
Getting a good ^xFFFF77Conquest Lamellar^7 is massive for your survivability, and in 3.27 this is easier than ever before. You can source this from the Genesis Tree, buy a Fractured base and craft (these are relatively cheap this league) or just find one that fits your needs buy it. If you already have ^xAF6025Rakiata's Dance^7 and 6 linking your new body armour would be expensive you can just run a single 6 link in your weapon and put off the second 6 link til you build up your stash again. This shouldn't be a problem this league, but might be an issue in the future when 6 links aren't as common as they are in 3.27.
Clusters are great but I would wait to put them in until you have good enough gear that you can trim some of the fat off the tree. Getting high res on your gear allows you to save a bunch of points, getting more Suppression on gear saves points, you have options here.
For ^xE05030Smite^7 links... check whether ^x7070FFIncreased Critical Strikes ^7or ^x7070FFIncreased Critical Damage^7 is better for you. If ^x7070FFTrinity ^7won't proc then you can run both of these or ^x7070FFAdded Lightning Damage^7
^xFFFF77Grafts^7 3.27's borrowed power comes in the form of Grafts. ^xFFFF77Storming Eshgraft^7 feels mandatory, and with either CDR or Skill Effect Duration your uptime is virtually permanent. I prefer CDR here because it allows me to move the ring more frequently. For your second graft you have some options, but I think you want a Tulgraft as they can roll "Non Vaal Strike Skills target 1 addional nearby enemy." I opted for the ^xFFFF77Aegis Tulgraft^7 to get some extra defense and have a skill with high uptime, but you could also use a ^xFFFF77Squalling Tulgraft^7 for a nice damage boost. Given that Slayer is already so good at pumping out damage I probably wouldn't do this, but if you were playing Champion I think there would be more merit to this.
^xFFFF77Lategame Optimization
^7Taking this build further you have a few options. The simplest optimzations would be adding ^x70FF70Max Frenzy Charges^7 and switching out ^xAF6025Impact^7 for ^xAF6025Masterful Form^7^7. You could also try something like ^xAF6025Call of the Brotherhood^7 and ^xAF6025Heatshiver^7 for some conversion shenanigans. You could also swap over to Champion and stack ^xE05030Fortification^7 ^7with ^xAF6025Celestial Brace^7. I haven't tested this in 3.27 yet but the ^xAF6025Rakiata's Dance^7 variant of this build didn't use ^xAF6025Doryani's Prototype^7 and so would still retain most of its power in this league. I have a video for this guide on my YouTube, and I'd be willing to update if for 3.27 if there was some interest. Facetanking Ubers with a 2 handed sword feels pretty damn good.
^xFFFF77Map Regexes ^7Prior to getting level 5 ^xE05030Awakened Elemental Damage with Attacks^7 we can't run Elemental Reflect. Picking this up is a really nice QoL but not a priority until your gear is looking solid. The "Safe" Regexes include mods that we can run but that are likely to get us killed. I use these regexes when I'm focused on levelling, but if you're not concerned with Experience you can use the "Risky" regexes and open up your pool of runnable maps.
For the T17 mod regexes I have left in quite a few annoying mods even in the "Safe" regexes. I would encourage you to check out PoE.re and make your own regex if you find that mine have too many map mods that annoy you. At the end of the day we're here to have fun and if things like Drowning Orbs are taking away from that there's no problem avoiding those mods. The reason I don't is just to keep my map pool as open as possible.
Before ^xE05030Awakened Elemental Damage with Attacks^7 T16 Safe - "!er,|eec|tal d|o al" T16 Risky - "!eec|tal d" T17 Safe - "!o al|'v|teal|om$|f el|net|ever" T17 Risky - "!om$|f el|ever"
After ^xE05030Awakened Elemental Damage with Attacks^7 T16 Safe - "!er,|eec|o al" T16 Risky - "!eec" T17 Safe - "!o al|'v|teal|om$|ever" T17 Risky - "!om$|ever"
Hopefully this helps some of you have a good time!
